context.assumption: Existing tests pass. Preserve existing public APIs unless a task explicitly says otherwise.
context.priority: Improve scientifically robust dataset search through corpus expansion, graph reasoning, compatibility scoring, validation, and human relevance calibration.

non_negotiables.item: All new features must use deterministic local fixtures by default.
Every ranking change needs a benchmark or trace artifact.
Graph and label claims must retain provenance and confidence.
Promotion to default retrieval must be intent-specific and gated.

agent.name: Claude
agent.operating_style: Use for cross-module prototypes, artifact generation, fixtures, reports, UI/API wiring, and benchmark loops.
agent.prompt_template: You are working in the neural-search repo. Assume tests pass. Implement the assigned feature end to end with deterministic fixtures, unit tests, an integration test, and a markdown/json report if search quality changes. Do not remove existing public APIs. Preserve hard-negative behavior. Run the smallest relevant tests and affected benchmark/report command. Summarize changed files, metrics, risks, and follow-up tasks.

task.title: Sparse plus RRF prototype
task.scope: Add lexical candidate generation and reciprocal rank fusion behind config.
task.acceptance: No hard-negative regression; benchmark comparison report emitted.

task.title: Graph metapath explanations
task.scope: Wire metapath templates into result graph_context and why_matched explanations.
task.acceptance: Search results show ranked paths with edge evidence and confidence.

task.title: Corpus expansion sprint
task.scope: Add fixture-backed records for undercovered source families and regenerate normalized records, graph, embeddings, reports, and benchmarks.
task.acceptance: Coverage plan improves and reports separate corpus gaps from ranking gaps.

task.title: Human review workflow surfaces
task.scope: Expose review queue, relevance labels, and card QA through API and frontend surfaces.
task.acceptance: Human label files roundtrip and calibration reports can consume them.

task.title: Scientific readiness dashboard
task.scope: Improve readiness reports with source balance, graph gaps, weak paper links, unresolved placeholders, and promotion blockers.
task.acceptance: JSON, markdown, and API endpoint outputs are tested.

agent.name: Codex
agent.operating_style: Use for narrow typed modules, isolated algorithms, edge-case tests, API contracts, and regression safety.
agent.prompt_template: You are working in the neural-search repo. Implement only the requested feature slice. Keep the diff small and typed. Add focused tests before broad integration changes. Do not refactor unrelated modules. Preserve existing behavior and public APIs unless explicitly instructed. Use deterministic fixtures and avoid network calls. Report files changed, tests added, exact behavior implemented, and assumptions.

task.title: Entity resolver
task.acceptance: Canonicalizes dataset IDs, source IDs, DOI/OpenAlex IDs, aliases, and duplicate records with deterministic conflict output.

task.title: Metapath scorer core
task.acceptance: Given graph plus template, returns ranked paths with edge confidence propagation and provenance.

task.title: Compatibility scorer
task.acceptance: Computes same, complementary, translational, analysis-compatible, and contrastive context scores.

task.title: Sparse retrieval index
task.acceptance: Builds deterministic BM25-like index over normalized records with field boosts and stable scores.

task.title: RRF fusion utility
task.acceptance: Fuses candidate lists with stable ties, source annotations, and explainable fusion contributions.

task.title: Calibration metrics
task.acceptance: Computes reliability bins and confidence curves from human relevance labels.

task.title: Graph QA gates
task.acceptance: Detects orphan nodes, weak links, missing required edge types, unresolved placeholders, and invalid confidence ranges.

task.title: Latent signature schema
task.acceptance: Defines versioned event-aligned signatures with dimensionality, extractor, source file, and QC metadata.

validation_gates.gate: Unit tests for parser, aliases, constraints, graph IDs, field scorers, and new modules.
Benchmark suites for demo, real corpus, adversarial queries, and data-form-specific queries.
Hard-negative violations must stay zero or not increase.
Human label minimums must be met before default promotion.
Search traces must exist for benchmark regressions.
Release summary must list blockers and rollback notes.
